reports.natureOfReport: Section 357 of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 (EPAct) directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct an inventory and analysis of oil and natural gas resources contained within the submerged lands of the United States (U.S.) Outer Continental Shelf (OCS). reports.title: National Institute on Disability, Independent Living, Research and Rehabilitation (NIDILRR) 2024-2028 Long-Range Plan
reports.organization: Department of Health and Human Services - Administration for Community Living
reports.legalAuthority: P.L. 113-128, Section 433(8) of The Workforce Innovation and Opportunities Act of 2014
reports.natureOfReport: The NIDILRR Long Range Plan (LRP) presents a 5-year agenda that will advance the field of applied disability, independent living, and rehabilitation research and development (R&D). This LRP will continue to emphasize scientific rigor and the need for research that is highly relevant to the lives of people with disabilities. The LRP emphasizes the inclusion of people with disabilities as critical to advancing both the rigor and relevance of NIDILRR-funded work. The LRP builds on the 2018-2023 LRP

reports.title: 2023 Report to Congress on the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking
reports.organization: Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
reports.legalAuthority: This Report to Congress on the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking (RTC) is required by the Sober Truth on Preventing (STOP) Underage Drinking Act (Pub. L. 109-422), which was enacted by Congress in 2006 and reauthorized in December 2022 as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 (Pub. L.117-328)
reports.natureOfReport: The 2022 RTC summarizes the status of the latest scientific research regarding adolescent alcohol use, describes the characteristics and consequences of underage drinking, and outlines the comprehensive efforts of the federal government to address the problem. The RTC features updated epidemiological data about the scope of the underage drinking problem in America and an updated chapter on the evaluation of the national media campaign, “Talk. They Hear You.”
reports.whenExpected: By December 31 of each year published

reports.title: 2022 Report to Congress on the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking
reports.agency: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
reports.organization: Department of Health and Human Services - Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
reports.legalAuthority: This Report to Congress on the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking (RTC) is required by the Sober Truth on Preventing (STOP) Underage Drinking Act (Pub. L. 109-422), which was enacted by Congress in 2006 and reauthorized in December 2022 as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 (Pub. L.117-328)
reports.natureOfReport: The 2022 RTC summarizes the status of the latest scientific research regarding adolescent alcohol use, describes the characteristics and consequences of underage drinking, and outlines the comprehensive efforts of the federal government to address the problem. The RTC features updated epidemiological data about the scope of the underage drinking problem in America and an updated chapter on the evaluation of the national media campaign, “Talk. They Hear You.”
reports.whenExpected: By December 31 of each year published

reports.title: 2022 State Performance and Best Practices for the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking
reports.agency: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
reports.organization: Department of Health and Human Services - Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
reports.legalAuthority: State Performance & Best Practices for the Prevention and Reduction of Underage Drinking Report (SPBP) is required by the Sober Truth on Preventing (STOP) Underage Drinking Act (Pub. L. 109-422), which was enacted by Congress in 2006 and reauthorized in December 2022 as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 (Pub. L.117-328)
reports.natureOfReport: The 2022 SPBP Report is intended to provide guidance to decision-makers about how to identify and select the intervention(s) that will best serve their state or community, as required by the STOP Act. The STOP Act also requires that the ICCPUD include in the report measures of states’ use of best practices in preventing underage drinking

reports.title: Highly Erodible Land Conservation and Wetland Conservation Determinations Prepared by the Natural Resources Conservation Service for the period October 1, 2021 through September 30, 2022, Fiscal Year (FY) 2022
reports.organization: Department of Agriculture - Natural Resources Conservation Service
reports.legalAuthority: Section 1241(c) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (as amended by Section 2602 of the Agricultural Act of 2014)
reports.natureOfReport: Provides a description of the extent to which the requests for HELC and WC determinations are being addressed in a timely manner. The report presents statistics for all 50 States, the Caribbean Area (Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands), and the Pacific Islands Area related to the completion of HELC and WC technical determinations. Also included are the number of determination requests that are outstanding more than 1 year since the date on which the requests were received from the producer
